semanticCore: Towering, lofty, high, proud, towering mountains, or rising skyward

keyDerivations.meaning: Towering, lofty, high, proud, or elevated

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا رَوَاسِيَ شَامِخَاتٍ وَأَسْقَيْنَاكُمْ مَاءً فُرَاتًا (77:27): And We made therein firm, towering/lofty mountains and gave you sweet water to drink.
summaryEn: The root refers to being extremely high or towering (Shamkh), describing the majestic, towering mountains that anchor the earth and store fresh water for human consumption.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الارتفاع الشاهق والشموخ والترفع، والجبال العالية الذرى (شمخ)، واصفاً الجبال الراسيات المانعة للأرض بالـ (شامخات).
rawText: جَبَلٌ شَامِخٌ A high, or lofty, mountain; (S, A, L;) as also 🡻شَمَّاخٌ [but in an intensive sense, i. e. a very high or lofty mountain]: (L:) pl. جِبَالٌ شَوَامِخُ (S, A) and شُمَّخٌ. (A.) === Hence, شَامِخٌ signifies also Proud; (L, TA;) elevating the nose, from pride, or disdain: [like زَامِخٌ:] pl. شُمَّخٌ: (L, K:) and 🡻شَمَّاخٌ a man who magnifies, or exalts, himself much; or elevates his nose much, from pride. (L.) === And أُنُوفٌ شُمَّخٌ [Noses elevated, from pride]; like زُمَّخٌ. (S.) === And نَسَبٌ شَامِخٌ ‡ [A proud, or high, lineage]. (TA.) شَمَخَ 1 , (S, L, K,) aor. ﹹ, inf. n. شُمُوخٌ, (L,) It (a mountain) was high, or lofty. (S, L, K.) === Hence, (Har p. 442,) شَمَخَ بِأَنْفِهِ, (S, A, L, K,) and شَمَخَ أَنْفُهُ, aor. and inf. n. as above, (L,) He (a man) magnified, or exalted, himself; was proud; (S, A, L, K;) behaved proudly, or disdainfully; elevated his nose, from pride. (L.) [See also زَمَخَ.] شَمَّاخٌ : see what follows, in two places. مَفَازَةٌ شَمُوخٌ A desert, or waterless desert, farextending; (K;) as also زمُوخٌ. (TA.) === See also شَمَخٌ. نِيَّةٌ شَمَخٌ (L, K) and 🡻شَمُوخٌ (L) A distant, far-reaching, or far-aiming, intention, purpose, or design; [in an action or a journey &c.;] (L, K;) as also نيَّةٌ زَمَخٌ and زَمُوخٌ. (L.)
